rules.title: Link target resolves
rules.description: Every derived_from, allocates, verifies, governing_adrs, supersedes, and superseded_by target resolves to an existing artifact in the corpus. Unresolved targets are validation errors, never warnings.

rules.title: Scope reference resolves
rules.description: Every scope reference (scope, parent_scope, scope_tags, affected_scopes) resolves to a scope present in the scope tree.

rules.title: Every requirement is allocated
rules.description: Every requirement at a non-leaf scope is allocated (via that scope's Architecture decomposition_child.allocates) to at least one child component. Unallocated requirements represent untraced intent.

rules.title: Every architecture child allocates a requirement
rules.description: Every child in an Architecture decomposition allocates at least one requirement. No orphan components — components without a requirement have no justification for existing.

rules.title: Every requirement and DD contract is verified
rules.description: Every requirement, and every Detailed Design public-interface contract, has at least one verifying test case (some TestSpec case with a matching verifies entry). Covers the design-test-coupling axiom (TARGET §3 principle 10).

rules.title: Every test case has non-empty verifies
rules.description: Every TestSpec case declares at least one verifies target. Tests without traceable intent are either misplaced or testing unspecified behaviour. The anti-orphan rule (TARGET §5.3 TestSpec).
rules.note: Also enforced inline by test-spec.schema.json (artifact-level envelope requires verifies minItems 1; $defs/test_case.verifies also minItems 1).

rules.title: Leaf has Detailed Design and TestSpec
rules.description: Every leaf scope in the scope tree carries both a Detailed Design artifact and a TestSpec artifact. Leaves without either are incomplete.

rules.title: Non-leaf has Requirements, Architecture, TestSpec
rules.description: Every non-leaf scope (root or branch) carries Requirements, Architecture, and TestSpec artifacts. Missing any of the three breaks the per-layer pattern (TARGET §8.1).

rules.title: ADR consequence is anchored
rules.description: Every ADR consequence is either satisfied by a requirement at the ADR's own scope (propagation-by-requirement) or referenced as a constraint by a child artifact via governing_adrs (propagation-by-reference). No dangling consequences.
rules.note: Implements the hybrid technical-selection propagation rule (TARGET §5.3 ADR, §9).

rules.title: No circular derived_from
rules.description: The derived_from graph across all spec artifacts is acyclic. A cycle indicates upstream / downstream confusion.

rules.title: No circular supersedes
rules.description: The supersedes / superseded_by graph across ADRs is acyclic. A cycle implies an ADR replaces one of its own ancestors.

rules.title: No reconstructed on human-only fields
rules.description: recovery_status value 'reconstructed' is banned on human-only fields: ADR context / alternatives_considered / rationale / consequences; Requirements rationale; all Product Brief content; Detailed Design Overview (intent). Allowed states on those fields are verified or unknown only. No-fabrication rule (TARGET §3 principle 7, §8.2).
rules.note: Enforced inline by each per-artifact schema on the specific field keys (PB scalar + map bans; Requirements rationale_recovery_status enum; ADR four-key map ban; DD overview map-key ban). This catalog rule documents the cross-artifact principle.

rules.title: Quality Bar cascade
rules.description: Upstream Quality Bar failure blocks downstream approval. A leaf artifact cannot pass review if any ancestor scope has unresolved Quality Bar items. Prevents leaf-level rubber-stamping on top of a broken parent (TARGET §6).
rules.note: Enforcement requires Quality Bar YAML — Phase 3 task #3 (container shape) and task #12 (per-artifact extraction). Listed here so the cross-artifact rule inventory is complete.
